question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit topic messages at a rate that causes the topic_message table to grow faster than the daily prune() job can remove data, given that prune() runs with fixedDelayString defaulting to one day, resulting in unbounded disk growth that eventually causes a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batchPeriod logic in RecordFileIterator to cause prune() to be called with the same consensusTimestamp threshold repeatedly in a tight loop, generating excessive DELETE statements that saturate the database connection pool and starve the importer of database connections?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit topic messages with message byte arrays containing patterns that trigger PostgreSQL's TOAST compression to produce larger-than-input output, causing the topic_message table to consume more disk space than expected and accelerating disk exhaustion beyond what the retention job can handle?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the JPQL DELETE in prune() does not specify a transaction isolation level, allowing a dirty read or phantom read scenario where newly inserted topic messages are included in the DELETE range, causing data loss that makes the gRPC subscription service return incomplete message sequences?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit topic messages with chunkNum greater than chunkTotal, causing the importer's chunk validation logic to throw an exception that prevents the entire record file from being processed, effectively halting the importer and causing all subsequent prune() calls to operate on stale data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a row count limit in prune() to cause a single DELETE statement to generate a transaction log entry so large that it exceeds PostgreSQL's max_wal_size configuration, forcing a checkpoint that stalls all database writes for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit topic messages with runningHashVersion values outside the expected range, causing the importer to log excessive error messages that fill the log storage volume and exhaust disk space, preventing the database from writing WAL entries and crashing PostgreSQL?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between the @Modifying annotation and Spring's EntityManager flush mode to cause prune() to flush all pending entity changes before executing the DELETE, creating a massive flush operation that consumes all available database connections and causes a connection pool exhaustion DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit topic messages with topicId values that map to the same database partition, causing partition-level lock contention between prune() DELETE operations and concurrent INSERT operations, resulting in lock wait timeouts that crash the importer's record processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() returns an int count of deleted rows, and if this count overflows Integer.MAX_VALUE due to an extremely large deletion, cause arithmetic overflow in RetentionJob's counter aggregation that produces incorrect metrics and masks the true scale of the DoS condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit topic messages with consensusTimestamp values clustered around the retention boundary to cause the RecordFileIterator's batchPeriod logic to generate an extremely large number of prune() iterations, each acquiring and releasing database transactions, exhausting the connection pool and causing a DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a RETURNING clause or row-count cap in the prune() DELETE to cause the database to generate an enormous result set internally that consumes all available shared_buffers memory in PostgreSQL, degrading performance for all other queries?
